    Home › Compare › Boeing 777 vs Boeing 787

    Boeing 777 vs Boeing 787

    Both aircraft at true relative scale — against a 1.8 m person and a double-decker bus.

    Wingspan and overall height are drawn to true scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft page. Compare any other aircraft in the live tool →

    The numbers

    Side by side

    Boeing 777Boeing 787DifferenceBigger
    Length73.86 m68.28 m5.58 mBoeing 777 +8%
    Wingspan64.80 m60.12 m4.68 mBoeing 777 +8%
    Height18.50 m17.02 m1.48 mBoeing 777 +9%
    MTOW352 t254 t98 tBoeing 777 +38%
    Typical seats39633660Boeing 777 +18%
    Range13,649 km11,730 km1,919 kmBoeing 777 +16%
    Max speed950 km/h954 km/h4 km/hBoeing 787
    The verdict

    Which is actually bigger?

    Same manufacturer, same job description, twenty years apart. The 777 is bigger in every dimension and carries far more people; the 787 flies nearly as far on dramatically less fuel and opened routes too thin for a 777 to ever fill. It is the clearest illustration on this site of what composites and modern engines actually bought aviation — not more speed, not more size, but more range per seat.

    The Boeing 777 is the longer of the two, at 73.86 m against 68.28 m — a difference of 5.58 m. It also has the greater wingspan, 4.68 m wider, so it is the bigger aircraft on both measures.

    The world's largest twinjet — the long-haul workhorse. The aircraft that reinvented long-haul economics. The silhouettes above are drawn at true relative scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft's page — same metres, same pixels, no artistic licence.

    On capacity, the Boeing 777 carries more people in a typical layout — 396 against 336.

    Boeing 777

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
